refnamediv.refpurpose: The sequences available in the Low Discrepancy toolbox.

refsection.title: Sequences
refsection.para: Several sequences are available in the Low Discrepancy toolbox. Each number sequence has its own properties, this is why the goal of this page is to detail the properties of these sequences. Each sequence can be configured with the -method option.

refsection.title: Classes of sequence
refsection.para: All Low Discrepancy sequences are designed to be able to produce real values uniform in the [0,1[^s interval, where s is the dimension of the space.

The generators in this class tries to produces numbers which favor the convergence of a Monte-Carlo simulation by reducing the discrepancy. These generators may be used, for example, in the Monte-Carlo based numerical integration of a function.
The list of generators available in this toolbox and producing low discrepancy sequences is the following : vandercorput, halton, faure, gslhalton, gslreversehalton, gslsobol, gslniederreiter-base-2.

refsection.title: Sources of Sequences
refsection.para: Although the random number toolbox provides the same interface for all generators, what happens behind the scenes is not the same, depending on the sequence. Two main sources are used in this toolbox.

listitem.para: Scripts are provided with this toolbox to implement both low-discrepancy sequences (such as Halton for instance). These sequences may be slow if a large number of data is to be generated, but may provide greater flexibility for some users.
These sequences are based on compiled C source code and should be as fast as possible.

refsection.title: Overview of sequences
refsection.para: In this section, we git the list of sequences which are provided in this component.

listitem.para: The Reverse Halton sequence of Vandewoestyne and Cools.
Add missing sequences in this page.
